member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.IEyeStateDetector.IsOpen(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24})
member.summary: Determines if the eye represented in the given image is open.
member.param: An image of an eye. The image should be cropped to contain only the region of interest (the eye).
member.returns: Returns 'true' if the eye in the image is determined to be open, and 'false' if it is closed.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.IFaceDetector.DetectFaces(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24})
member.summary: Detects faces in the provided image. When supported by the implementation, facial landmark points are also extracted for each detected face. The number and interpretation of landmark points are specific to the implementation.
member.remarks: - The quality of the input image can affect the accuracy of face detection. High-resolution images where faces are clearly visible tend to yield better results. - Facial landmark points refer to specific features on a face, such as center points or corners of the eyes, nose tip, and mouth corners. These can be used for alignment, emotion analysis, etc. - Bounding boxes represent the regions within the image where faces are detected. - Confidence values indicate the probability that the detected region actually contains a face. - It's advisable to consult the documentation of the specific implementation to understand how to interpret and use the landmark points effectively.
member.param: The input image which may contain one or more human faces.
member.returns: A collection of results, where each result contains the bounding box, facial landmark coordinates (if supported), and a confidence score for each face detected in the image.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.IFaceEmbeddingsGenerator.GenerateEmbedding(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24})
member.summary: Generates an embedding vector for the given aligned face image. The embedding vectors produced by this function can be used to compare faces; vectors for images of the same person will be closer to each other in the embedding space. The choice of similarity metric for comparing the vectors depends on the specific implementation and the nature of the problem. Common similarity metrics include cosine similarity and euclidean distance.
member.remarks: Depending on the specific face recognition implementation, it may be important to properly align the input image. How the alignment is done depends on the model used for the implementation.
member.param: An aligned image of a face.
member.returns: An embedding vector (in a high-dimensional space) that represents the unique features of the given face. This vector can be compared with others to determine if multiple faces belong to the same person.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.IFaceEmbeddingsGenerator.AlignFaceUsingLandmarks(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF})
member.summary: Aligns the given face image based on the provided facial landmarks. Alignment is specific to the model used for implementation. This method operates in-place and thus modifies the given image.
member.param: The image of the face to be aligned. The given image will be mutated.

A read-only list of points representing facial landmarks. These landmarks usually include the coordinates of features like eyes, nose, mouth, etc. Which landmarks are expected in which order depends on the implementation.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.IFaceLandmarksDetector.DetectLandmarks(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24})
member.summary: Extract facial landmark points from a properly cropped image of a face. The amount and meaning of the points depends on the model used for implementation.
member.param: Cropped image of a face.
member.returns: Facial landmark coordiantes.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.IFaceLandmarksDetector.GetLeftEyeCenter(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF})
member.summary: Returns the center point of the left eye, based on the given landmarks.
member.param: Landmark points as returned by .
member.returns: Center point of the left eye.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.IFaceLandmarksDetector.GetRightEyeCenter(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF})
member.summary: Returns the center point of the right eye, based on the given landmarks.
member.param: Landmark points as returned by .
member.returns: Center point of the right eye.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Applications.BlurFaces(FaceAiSharp.IFaceDetector,SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24},System.Single)
member.summary: Blurs all faces that are found by the given face detector. Modifies the given image (destructive).
member.param: The face detector to use.
The image to search and blur faces in.
Factor to determine sigma for the gaussian blur. sigma = max(height, width) / factor.
member.returns: The number of faces that have been blurred.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Applications.CountFaces(FaceAiSharp.IFaceDetector,SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24})
member.summary: Counts the number of faces in the given image.
member.param: The detector used to search for faces.
The input image.
member.returns: The number of faces found.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Applications.CountEyeStates(FaceAiSharp.IFaceDetectorWithLandmarks,FaceAiSharp.IEyeStateDetector,SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24},System.Single)
member.summary: Counts the number of faces as well as opened and closed eyes in the given image. Note that the eye state will only be estimated for eyes with an edge length >= 16. Thus it is possible that Faces > OpenEyes + ClosedEyes.
member.param: The detector used to search for faces.
The eye state detector.
The input image.

The higher the value given is, the smaller the boxes around the eyes will be. Square eye box length = distance between eyes / * 2. 3.0 seems to be a suitable value for typical eye sizes and some additional space around the eyes.
member.returns: The numbers of faces, open and closed eyes found.
member.exception: Thrown if the face detections by do not include facial landmarks.

member.name: F:FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGenerator.ExpectedLandmarkPositionsInsightface
member.summary: Points from https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface/blob/c7bf2048e8947a6398b4b8bda6d1958138fdc9b5/python-package/insightface/utils/face_align.py.

member.name: F:FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGenerator.ExpectedLandmarkPositionsOnnxZoo
member.summary: Points from https://github.com/onnx/models/blob/8e893eb39b131f6d3970be6ebd525327d3df34ea/vision/body_analysis/arcface/dependencies/arcface_inference.ipynb.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGenerator.#ctor(FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGeneratorOptions,Microsoft.ML.OnnxRuntime.SessionOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Provide a path to the ONNX model file and customize the behaviour of .
to customize OnnxRuntime's behaviour.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGenerator.#ctor(System.Byte[],FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGeneratorOptions,Microsoft.ML.OnnxRuntime.SessionOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: An ONNX model containing the ResNet100 model with 1x3x112x112 input dimensions.
Options to customize the behaviour of . If options.ModelPath is set, it is ignored. The model provided in takes precedence.
to customize OnnxRuntime's behaviour.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGenerator.AlignFaceUsingLandmarks(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF},System.Int32)
member.summary: Align the given image in the way ArcFace was trained. Applies an affine transformation to move the facial landmark points to expected positions. This method operates in-place and thus modifies the given image.
member.param: The image of the face to be aligned. The given image will be mutated.
Five facial landmark points. In this order: - left eye center - right eye center - nose tip - left mouth corner - right mouth corner.
ArcFace typically expects 112x112 inputs. Specifying another edge size might be useful for custom models or to manually analyze the resulting aligned faces.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGeneratorOptions.ModelPath
member.summary: Gets the path to the ONNX file that contains the ResNet100 model with 1x3x112x112 input dimensions.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ArcFaceEmbeddingsGeneratorOptions.AutoResizeInputToModelDimensions
member.summary: Resize the image to dimensions supported by the model if required. This detector throws an exception if this is set to false and an image is passed in unsupported dimensions.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.EuclideanDistance(System.Single[],System.Single[])
member.summary: Calculate euclidean distance between to arbitrary length vectors. Not optimized. See e.g. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.runtime.intrinsics.vector256-1?view=net-7.0 for possible optimizations. Also, this could use Generic Math if it was .Net 7. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/generics/math.
member.param: Vector of single precision floating point numbers.
Vector of single precision floating point numbers. Length needs to match the other vector's length.
member.returns: Euclidean distance.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.EuclideanSimilarity(System.Single[],System.Single[])
member.summary: Gets a similarity measure between two points based on euclidean distance.
member.param: The first vector to be compared.
The second vector to be compared.
member.returns: A similarity measure between x and y.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.CosineDistance(System.Single[],System.Single[])
member.summary: Computes the distance d(x,y) between points and .
member.param: The first point, x.
The second point, y.
member.returns: A single-precision value representing the distance d(x,y) between and according to cosine similarity.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.CosineSimilarity(System.Single[],System.Single[])
member.summary: Gets a similarity measure between two points based on cosine similarity.
member.param: The first vector to be compared.
The second vector to be compared.
member.returns: A similarity measure between x and y.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.Dot(System.Single[],System.Single[])
member.summary: Returns the dot product of two vectors.
member.param: The first vector.
The second vector.
member.returns: The dot product.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.ScaleToRotationAngleInvariantCropArea(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Rectangle)
member.summary: Gets an area that contains all pixels that could be needed if the given crop-rectangle should be rotated by any angle.
member.param: A crop rectangle.
member.returns: A larger rectangle.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.GetMinimumSupersetSquare(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Rectangle)
member.summary: Returns a square that contains the given rectangle in it's middle.
member.param: This rectangle's area should be in the center of the returned square.
member.returns: A square shaped area.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.GetHScaleFactor(System.Numerics.Matrix3x2)
member.summary: Calculates the horizontal scale factor (X Axis) of a transform that is applied by multiplying the given matrix.
member.param: Input matrix.
member.returns: The horizontal scale factor/x axis.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.GetVScaleFactor(System.Numerics.Matrix3x2)
member.summary: Calculates the vertical scale factor (Y Axis) of a transform that is applied by multiplying the given matrix.
member.param: Input matrix.
member.returns: The vertical scale factor/y axis.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.GeometryExtensions.GetAlignmentAngle(SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF,SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF)
member.summary: Calculates the angle in degrees by which an image needs to be rotated so that the line formed by the given points (left and right) becomes horizontal, with the left point being to the left of the right point.
member.param: The PointF representing the left point.
The PointF representing the right point.
member.returns: The angle in degrees by which the image should be rotated to align the given points horizontally.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.ImageExtensions.EnsureProperlySized``1(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image,SixLabors.ImageSharp.Processing.ResizeOptions,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns an image that matches a defined size and PixelFormat. If the given image already conforms to this specification, it is returned directly. If a conversion is required the pixels of the input image will only be copied exactly once. If a copy is created, the value returned equals the value in the same tuple. If the passed-in is returned directly, the value returned is null. Thus, you should always use the in a using block or using var declaration.

example.code: (var img, var disp) = image.GetProperlySized<Rgb24>(resizeOptions); using var usingDisp = disp;

member.typeparam: The pixel format the returned image should have.
member.param: The image to return in a proper shape.
How to resize the input, if required.
If an actual Resize operation is required to match the spec, throw.
member.returns: An instance sticking to the spec.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.ImageToTensorExtensions.ToTensor(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24})
member.summary: Efficiently converts one image to an input tensor. Preprocesses for {r, g, b}: r = (r * / 255) - 0.5.
member.param: The image to preprocess.
member.returns: A tensor containing the preprocessed image.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.ImageToTensorExtensions.ToTensor(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24},System.Single[],System.Single[])
member.summary: Efficiently converts one image to an input tensor. Preprocesses for {r, g, b}: r = (r * (1 / 255 * stddev.r)) - (mean.r / stddev.r). Thus, if you need simple r = r, you could pass stddev = 1/255 and mean = 0. Note that while it might seem unnecessary to use this method then, it's memory access is optimized and the conversion is almost two orders of magnitude faster than a simple approach.
member.param: The image to preprocess.
The rgb mean values used for normalization.
The rgb stddev values used for normalization.
member.returns: A tensor containing the preprocessed image.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.ImageToTensorExtensions.ToTensor(SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24},System.Single[],System.Single[],System.Int32[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: Efficiently converts one image to an input tensor. Preprocesses for {r, g, b}: r = (r * (1 / 255 * stddev.r)) - (mean.r / stddev.r). Thus, if you need simple r = r, you could pass stddev = 1/255 and mean = 0. Note that while it might seem unnecessary to use this method then, it's memory access is optimized and the conversion is almost two orders of magnitude faster than a simple approach.
member.param: The image to preprocess.
The rgb mean values used for normalization.
The rgb stddev values used for normalization.

The dimensions the created tensor should have. Might throw exceptions if memory access fails due to invalid dimensions. The first dimension's value will be set to 1 no matter what you pass in, as this method processes exactly one picture.
Swap the image's channels during conversion. RGB -> BGR. Some models expect their input data in BGR instead of RGB.
member.returns: A tensor containing the preprocessed image.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Extensions.ImageToTensorExtensions.ImageToTensor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image{SixLabors.ImageSharp.PixelFormats.Rgb24}},System.Single[],System.Single[],System.Int32[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: Efficiently converts images to an input tensor for batch processing. Preprocesses for {r, g, b}: r = (r * (1 / 255 * stddev.r)) - (mean.r / stddev.r). Thus, if you need simple r = r, you could pass stddev = 1/255 and mean = 0.
member.param: The images to convert.
The rgb mean values used for normalization.
The rgb stddev values used for normalization.
The size of the tensor that the OnnxRuntime model is expecting, e.g. [1, 3, 224, 224].
Swap the image's channels during conversion. RGB -> BGR. Some models expect their input data in BGR instead of RGB.
member.returns: A tensor that contains the converted batch of images.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ImageCalculations.GetEyeBoxesFromCenterPoints(SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF,SixLabors.ImageSharp.PointF,System.Single)
member.summary: Estimates bounding boxes for eyes if just their center points are known. It assumes the size of the required boxes linearly depends on the distance of the eye center points.
member.param: The center point of the left eye.
The center point of the right eye.

The higher the value given is, the smaller the boxes around the eyes will be. Square eye box length = distance between eyes / * 2. 3.0 seems to be a suitable value for typical eye sizes and some additional space around the eyes.
member.returns: Calculated bounding boxes for the eyes.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Metrics.Auc(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.ValueTuple{System.Single,System.Boolean}})
member.summary: Calculate the "area under the ROC curve" value for the given estimations. AUC is a quality metric for a classifier. Higher is better. Below 0.5 is useless for a binary classifier.
member.param: A list of tuples of confidence values our model calculated with their the corresponding ground-truth values.
member.returns: The "area under the ROC curve" value for the given estimations.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Metrics.RocPoints(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.ValueTuple{System.Single,System.Boolean}})
member.summary: Generates the points needed to draw a ROC curve for the classifier that generated the estimations you pass as arguments.
member.param: A list of tuples of confidence values our model calculated with their the corresponding ground-truth values.
member.returns: Points to draw in a ROC curve.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.Metrics.FindThreshold(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.ValueTuple{System.Single,System.Boolean}})
member.summary: Finds an optimal threshold for a binary classifier. An optimal threshold is defined as the threshold that leads to the highest possible accuracy.
member.param: A list of tuples of confidence values our model calculated with their the corresponding ground-truth values.
member.returns: A threshold that leads to the highest possible accuracy.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.OpenVinoOpenClosedEye0001.#ctor(FaceAiSharp.OpenVinoOpenClosedEye0001Options,Microsoft.ML.OnnxRuntime.SessionOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Provide a path to the ONNX model file and customize the behaviour of .
to customize OnnxRuntime's behaviour.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.OpenVinoOpenClosedEye0001.#ctor(System.Byte[],FaceAiSharp.OpenVinoOpenClosedEye0001Options,Microsoft.ML.OnnxRuntime.SessionOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: OpenVino's open-closed-eye-0001/open_closed_eye.onnx model with 1x3x32x32 BGR input.
Options to customize the behaviour of . If options.ModelPath is set, it is ignored. The model provided in takes precedence.
to customize OnnxRuntime's behaviour.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.OpenVinoOpenClosedEye0001Options.ModelPath
member.summary: Gets the path to the onnx file that contains open-closed-eye-0001/open_closed_eye.onnx with 1x3x32x32 BGR input.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.OpenVinoOpenClosedEye0001Options.AutoResizeInputToModelDimensions
member.summary: Resize the image to dimensions supported by the model if required. This detector throws an exception if this is set to false and an image is passed in unsupported dimensions.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Memory.IMemoryCache,FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetectorOptions,Microsoft.ML.OnnxRuntime.SessionOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: An instance that is used internally by .
Provide a path to the ONNX model file and customize the behaviour of .
to customize OnnxRuntime's behaviour.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.#ctor(System.Byte[],Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Memory.IMemoryCache,FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetectorOptions,Microsoft.ML.OnnxRuntime.SessionOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: An scrfd onnx model that supports facial landmarks ("kps").
An instance that is used internally by .
Options to customize the behaviour of . If options.ModelPath is set, it is ignored. The model provided in takes precedence.
to customize OnnxRuntime's behaviour.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.NonMaxSupression(NumSharp.NDArray,System.Single)
member.summary: Filter out duplicate detections (multiple boxes describing roughly the same area) using non max suppression.
member.param: All detections with their scores.
Non max suppression threshold.
member.returns: Which detections to keep.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.IndicesOfElementsLargerThen(NumSharp.NDArray,System.Single)
member.summary: In real numpy this could be eg. np.where(scores>=thresh) or np.asarray(condition).nonzero().
member.param: The indices of this array's elements should be returned.
The threshold value. Exclusive.
member.returns: An NDArray contianing the indices.

member.name: T:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters
member.summary: Loosely on https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface/blob/e8c33fc91f60c28f088415864df9d200e11c4c30/python-package/insightface/model_zoo/scrfd.py#L88.
member.param: Input dimensions.
Name of the input tensor.
True if the model supports batching.
Probably "Feature Map Count", referring to the number of feature maps produced by the model.
Probably "Feature Stride Feature Pyramid Network", referring to the stride of the feature pyramid network.
True if the model supports facial landmarks.
Number of anchors.

member.name: M:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.#ctor(System.Nullable{SixLabors.ImageSharp.Size},System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32[],System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Loosely on https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface/blob/e8c33fc91f60c28f088415864df9d200e11c4c30/python-package/insightface/model_zoo/scrfd.py#L88.
member.param: Input dimensions.
Name of the input tensor.
True if the model supports batching.
Probably "Feature Map Count", referring to the number of feature maps produced by the model.
Probably "Feature Stride Feature Pyramid Network", referring to the stride of the feature pyramid network.
True if the model supports facial landmarks.
Number of anchors.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.InputSize
member.summary: Input dimensions.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.InputName
member.summary: Name of the input tensor.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.Batching
member.summary: True if the model supports batching.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.Fmc
member.summary: Probably "Feature Map Count", referring to the number of feature maps produced by the model.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.FeatStrideFpn
member.summary: Probably "Feature Stride Feature Pyramid Network", referring to the stride of the feature pyramid network.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.SupportsKps
member.summary: True if the model supports facial landmarks.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetector.ModelParameters.NumAnchors
member.summary: Number of anchors.

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetectorOptions.ModelPath
member.summary: Gets the path to the onnx file that contains the scrfd model that supports facial landmarks ("kps").

member.name: P:FaceAiSharp.ScrfdDetectorOptions.AutoResizeInputToModelDimensions
member.summary: Resize the image to dimensions supported by the model if required. This detector throws an exception if this is set to false and an image is passed in unsupported dimensions.
